reforms.description: The President announced two programmes aimed at increasing the share of renewable energy and developing oil substitutes. The first programme targets raising solar power and renewable resources from 6% to 20% of energy consumption by 2000, while the second focuses on directing the development of 2.5 mbd of oil substitutes from various sources by 1990.
reforms.package_name: Renewable Energy and Oil Substitutes Initiative
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives aim to enhance energy security and promote the use of renewable resources, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms were announced by the President, indicating their planned implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: While these reforms are significant in promoting renewable energy, they do not represent a structural overhaul of the energy policy framework.
reforms.source_quote: In June the President announced a programme aimed at increasing the share of solar power and energy from other renewable resources from the current 6 per cent of energy consumption to 20 per cent by the year 2000.

reforms.description: The Footwear Industry Revitalisation Programme was introduced to counteract the growing dependence of domestic demand on foreign supply, providing $56 million over three years to support domestic shoe production.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This programme aims to bolster domestic production in the footwear industry, which can enhance local employment and economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been implemented to support domestic shoe production.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme is significant in its support for a specific industry but does not represent a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: The purpose of this scheme is to counteract the growing dependence of domestic demand on foreign supply: between 1968 and 1977 domestic output of shoes (excluding rubber footwear) had fallen from about 620 million pairs a year to under 400 million.
